settings.title: A Two-Day Coastal Celebration
settings.summary: We ran a relaxed seaside ceremony followed by a beach barbecue the next day.
settings.description:
Aoife and Daniel had guests travelling a long way to the Dorset coast and wanted to make a weekend of it rather than one long day. With people coming from far afield, squeezing everything into a single afternoon felt like a waste of the journey everyone had made.

Two days, two moods

We split the celebration in two so each part could have its own character. Saturday was the ceremony at a clifftop venue, more formal and tied to a fixed time. Sunday was a beach barbecue with games and a bonfire, deliberately loose and built for people to drift in and out of.

The coastal logistics

Working on the coast adds variables you do not get inland, and we planned around all of them:

  • Tide times checked so the beach event sat in a safe, usable window
  • Permissions secured for the barbecue and bonfire on the shore
  • A foul-weather backup arranged for both days

Why it worked

Spreading the celebration across two days took the pressure off the schedule. Nothing had to be crammed in, so the ceremony could breathe and the barbecue could run at its own pace.

The real benefit was social. With the extra time, people actually got to talk to each other instead of rushing from one set piece to the next, and guests who had travelled a long way felt the trip was worth it.
